oai_dc:dc.dc:title: The importance of externships and how to choose them
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Dziuba, Monika
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: externships
students
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Third- and fourth-year veterinary students can gain and learn various experiences and skills from performing externships before heading into their first job after graduation. These ex­ternships can be an opportunity to evaluate potential job loca­tions, explore different disciplines in the field, and practice vet­erinary clinical skills. Additional effort during externships that can help to create successful experiences include making one-to-two SMART goals for each externship, determining mentor­ship style and needs, and receiving feedback. When selecting externships, students should search for beneficial experiences in multiple different fields of the industry, such as farm intern­ships, veterinary practices, industry companies, or university programs.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 190
